label: Five-year business survival rate
subtitle: Percentage of newly born businesses that survived for five years
description: This indicator shows the five-year survival rate of UK businesses born in 2019.
caveats:
A business is deemed to have survived for five years if it is active in terms of employment and/or turnover at any point during the fifth calendar year following its year of birth.

The five‑year business survival rate is calculated as the number of businesses that survive for five years as a proportion of businesses born in the start year.

Some areas are affected by large numbers of businesses registering or de-registering at the same postcode (such as virtual offices, company formation agents, or foreign online sellers), which can distort local survival rates. The [Multiple Business Registrations methodology paper](https://www.ons.gov.uk/businessindustryandtrade/business/activitysizeandlocation/articles/multiplebusinessregistrationsatasinglepostcodeuk/latest) explains this and provides tables identifying postcodes with 500 or more business births or deaths. These registrations cannot be removed from the Business Register, but users may wish to consider their impact when interpreting local figures.
